On the other question -- yes, it was an italicization thing. The '''Tremendous''' is a ship in the same class as the '''Courageous'''. Another one of my trivial imports from the Napoleonic era navy; their ''Tremendous'' was another 74-gun ship like the ''Courageaux'', a French ship captured in 1761 and used as the model for the ''Leviathan'' class. (I liked the Karath connection there, obviously!)}}</ref>

{{Quote|I am hopeful that we will be able to make Myrkr or the Ryyk Nebula, where we might reform with the ''Tremendous'' group arriving from [point of origin classified].|Admiral Saul Karath, in a dispatch to the Republic Navy [[Unidentified Admiral of the Fleet|Admiral of the Fleet]]|<ref name="serroco" />}}
The '''''Tremendous''''' was an [[Inexpugnable-class tactical command ship|''Inexpugnable''-class tactical command ship]] that served the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]] during the [[Mandalorian Wars]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] warrior clans. The [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] of a [[Tremendous group|battle group]] by the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[3963 BBY]], the ''Tremendous'' at one point patrolled the area of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Myrkr/Legends|Myrkr]] and the [[Ryyk Nebula/Legends|Ryyk Nebula]]. After the Mandalorian [[Battle of Serroco|devastation]] of the planet [[Serroco]], Republic [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Saul Karath]] retreated and attempted to link up with the ''Tremendous'' and its battle group, but his [[Courageous (Inexpugnable-class)|flagship]] was destroyed before he got the chance.

==Description==
An [[Inexpugnable-class tactical command ship|''Inexpugnable''-class tactical command ship]]<ref name="kotorcg" /><ref name="shipclass" /> manufactured by [[Rendili Hyperworks/Legends|Rendili Hyperworks]], the ''Tremendous'' featured a massive [[Vanjervalis Systems]] [[Computer/Legends|computer]] fitted to its frame that allowed it to serve as a mobile platform from which the actions and movements of an entire [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] could be coordinated. Up to sixty-four [[Hammerhead-class cruiser|''Hammerhead''-class cruisers]] could be connected to the ''Tremendous'' at any one [[Time/Legends|time]], and the dozens of [[Starship/Legends|ships]] gave the slow-moving ''Tremendous'' plenty of defense. Like other ''Inexpugnable''-class ships, the ''Tremendous'' featured the class's trademark "wishing-well" [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] configuration, where visual and electronic data was displayed [[Hologram/Legends|holographically]] over a transparent deck.<ref name="kotorcg" />

The ''Tremendous'' had a width of 3,100 [[Meter/Legends|meters]], and while the [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]] was able to enjoy the protection of its phalanx of Hammerhead [[Cruiser/Legends|cruisers]], it had an armament that included five double [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] batteries, five light turbolaser batteries, two [[Point-defense laser cannon/Legends|point-defense laser cannons]], and two [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] batteries, all of which combined required sixty-six [[Gunner/Legends|gunners]] to operate. The ''Courageous'' also could contain a sizable complement of ships, including up to 144 [[Aurek-class tactical strikefighter|''Aurek''-class tactical strikefighters]], twenty-four [[S-250 Chela-class starfighter|S-250 ''Chela''-class starfighters]], twenty-four [[Conductor-class short-haul landing craft|''Conductor''-class short-haul landing craft]], six [[Ministry-class orbital shuttle|''Ministry''-class orbital shuttles]], and other support craft. Equipped with a [[Navigation computer/Legends|navicomputer]], a Class 2 [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]], and a Class 10 unit as a backup, the ''Tremendous'' could hold 50,000 [[Metric ton/Legends|tons]] of cargo and two [[Standard year/Legends|years]]' worth of consumables. Though it was not available for sale, the ship carried an estimated value of 70 million [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]].<ref name="kotorcg" />

==History==
The ''Tremendous'' was active during the [[Mandalorian Wars]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] warrior clans, heading its own [[Tremendous group|battle group]] by [[3963 BBY]]. That year, after the Mandalorian [[Battle of Serroco|devastation]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Serroco]], [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Saul Karath]] retreated along with his [[Battle Group Serroco|battle group]] under Mandalorian pursuit. At the time, the ''Tremendous'' and its [[Flotilla/Legends|flotilla]] were located near the planet [[Myrkr/Legends|Myrkr]] and the [[Ryyk Nebula/Legends|Ryyk Nebula]], and Karath hoped to link up with them<ref name="serroco" />—however, Karath's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Courageous (Inexpugnable-class)|''Courageous'']], was run down and destroyed by the Mandalorians before he ever had the chance.<ref name="kotor17">[[Knights of the Old Republic 17|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 17]]</ref>

==Commanders and crew==
Like other ''Inexpugnable''-class ships, the ''Courageous'' could house up to 4,300 skilled crew and 2,000 [[Republic soldier|soldiers]].<ref name="kotorcg" />

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Tremendous'' was first mentioned in ''[[The Admiral's List: Remember Serroco! Edition]]'', an [[in-universe]] news report appearing on the last page of [[Knights of the Old Republic 16|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 16]], a [[Star Wars comic books|comic-book]] written by [[John Jackson Miller]], released by [[Dark Horse Comics]],<ref name="serroco" /> and released on [[May 2]], [[2007]].<ref name="DH">{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/14-397/Star-Wars-Knights-of-the-Old-Republic-16|text=Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #16}}</ref> Miller named the ''Tremendous'' after a ship from the [[Wiki:Napoleonic era|Napoleonic era]] [[Wiki:French Navy|French Navy]].<ref name="shipclass" />
